subject: Accounting
title: Employees and the Public
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Employees and the Public.

hook.text: Employees rarely read financial statements. But those numbers decide raises and job security.

buildup.text: A company's financial health determines whether it can hire, invest, or survive a downturn.

discovery.text: Public companies must share financials. Anyone—employees, journalists—can read them.

twist.text: Enron employees ignored red flags in public filings and lost their retirement savings.

climax.text: Financial literacy isn't just for accountants. It protects everyone who depends on a company.

punchline.text: The numbers affect everyone. Everyone should read them.

quiz.question: Why should employees care about financial statements?
options: They reveal company health and job security
